role.description: Role to assume with the provided token. Resources are created with this role instead of the original principal

service_registry.description: Service Registry to use for endpoint discovery

block.description: Managed Gateway --- Represents a connection resource.
Managed Gateway --- Represents a gateway resource.
Managed Gateway --- Represents a translation resource.

comments.description: You can optionally add comments to records to describe their intended usage

last_change_date.description: The date at which the record was last updated

tenant.description: The tenant that the record belongs to.

ttl.description: TTL, if not set, defaults to the zones TTL

type.description: Type of the record (i.e. A, CNAME, ...)

zone_name.description: Zone that this record belongs to

block.description: DNS --- Defines a DNS record.

created_by.description: The CRN of the user who created the VPC Project.

creation_date.description: The timestamp of the date when the VPC Project was created.

limits.description: The resource limits currently configured for this VPC Project.

name.description:
The user defined name used to construct the OpenStack project name with the schema `tenant-name`.  
By changing this value, the old project will be deleted and a new project with the new name will be created.

!> Changing this value will delete all resources in the VPC Project.

openstack_uuid.description: The uuid of the OpenStack Project.

project_comment.description:
A comment to describe what this VPC Project is used for.  
By changing this value, the old project will be deleted and a new project will be created.

!> Changing this value will delete all resources in the VPC Project.

tenant.description: The id of the tenant this VPC Project belongs to.

users.description: The list of users with access to the VPC Project. The list may only contains CRNs of human iam users.

block.description:
Dynamic Cloud --- Manage Dynamic Cloud VPC Projects lifecycle

This creates a Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) Project with the specified name and the optional comment. The parameter `users` can be used to specify which user should get access to the VPC Project.

!> Changing the `name` or `comment` will force the VPC Project to be recreated, i.e. all resources in the VPC Project will be deleted.

principal.description: The principal that the policy should be applied to. Can be applied to Users, ServiceUsers, and Roles.

service.description: The service name to provide permissions for.

custom.description: Provide a customized policy. If `custom` is set, it is preferred over `profile`

profile.description: Can be set to `reader`, or `full-access`. Applies either reader or administrative access to the service.

block.description:
You can choose either a profile or a custom policy.   
This block allows to setup policies using predefined profiles (`policy.profile`). Profile can either be `full-access` or `reader`, providing either administrative access to the service, or read-only access.  
If you want to manage policies on a find-grained basis, you can use `policy.custom` instead.

~> **Note:** `profile` and `custom` are not joined into a single policy. If both are provided, `custom` is preferred over `profile`, and `profile` will be ignored.

~> **Note:** Either `profile` or `custom` must be provided. Otherwise, this resource will fail.

.
IAM --- Generate a new IAM policy and attach it to users, service users and roles.

created_at.description: The initial creation date of the role.

created_by.description: The initial creator of the role.

description.description: Description for what the role is used for.

group.description: Group that the role belongs to.

name.description: Name of the role. The name of the role must be unique in your account.

tenant.description: Tenant that the role belongs to.

block.description: IAM --- Role with short-term credentials.

created_at.description: The initial creation date of the service user.

created_by.description: The initial creator of the service user.

description.description: Description for what the service user is used for.

group.description: Group that the service user belongs to.

name.description: Name of the service user. The name must be unique in your account.

tenant.description: Tenant that the service user belongs to.

block.description: IAM --- User with long-term credentials for automation purpose.

created_at.description: The initial creation date of the user.

created_by.description: The initial creator of the user.

description.description: Description for what the user is used for.

group.description: Group that the user belongs to.

name.description: Name of the user. The name must be unique in your account.

tenant.description: Tenant that the user belongs to.

block.description: IAM --- IAM user authenticated against the central CANCOM SSO.

IP Management --- IPAM host allows you to assign host ip-addresses from a network that must be enabled for host assignment. The network is identified by it's id (crn).  
You manage networks and enable them for host-assignments by using the portal or the network resource of this provider.
IP Management --- An IPAM instance.
IP Management --- IPAM networks are assigned by the service from supernets.
IP Management --- IPAM Supernets are larger aggregates that are further subnetted by the service.

availability_class.description: Name of the availability class. One of singleDc or multiDc.

bucket_name.description: Name of the bucket. The name must be unique GLOBALLY.

block.description: Object Storage --- Create and manage S3 buckets. Bucket names must be globally unique.

access_key_id.description: The access key of the user. Only included if `include_credentials` is set to true.

description.description: Description what the user is used for

include_credentials.description: Add the user credentials into the Terraform state. If set, `access_key_id` and `secret_access_key` will be populated into the state. Otherwise, an empty string will be written to the state. Please note that, since the credentials are only available once, manually regenerated credentials will not be synchronized into the state. If you must reroll the credentials, please consider recreating the user instead.

secret_access_key.description: Secret Access Key. Only included if `include_credentials` is set to true.

username.description: Name of the user. The name must be unique inside your account.

auto_scan.description: Only relevant on resource creation. Automatically starts a scan while creating the monitoring item. *This operation incurs a charge*

comment.description: Comment that provides more details about the item, for example what to do when an alarm is raised

contact_email_cancom.description: CANCOM contact person that is responsible to exchange the certificate.

contact_email_customer.description: Notification is send to the contact if an alarm is raised.

created_at.description: When was the monitoring item created.

domain_name.description: Domain name to monitor. A domain name can only be monitored once.

is_managed_by_cancom.description: This item is managed by CANCOM, and a CANCOM employee is responsible to renew the certificate. Can only be set by CANCOM employees.

last_updated_by.description: Who was the last principal to update the item.

minimum_grade.description: Alert grade. If the score falls below this score, an alarm is raised.

not_after.description: Information from the certificate, when it is about to expire.

protocol.description: The protocol to be monitored, i.e. `http`, `postgresql`, `smtp`, ...

ssl_scan_enabled.description: Are scans scheduled, or do we not schedule them at this moment?.

tenant.description: The tenant that created the monitoring item.

block.description: SSL Monitoring --- A SSL monitor item.

role.description: Role of the Machine - needs to match predefined roles.

services.description: Services deployed and delivered to machine

block.description:
Windows OS --- CANCOM Managed Windows Cloud Server deployment resource allows you to send metadata to CANCOM Windows Server management platform.

ID and customer number need do be assigned to a created virtual machine with CANCOM Windows Images and allows the auto-registration, installation and integration to customer environment.

errorhandling.description:
The errorhandling of deployment errors is configurable. If the output should be just warining and no interruption of the process is needed, please set the value to warning.

				!> For multi staged deployments the value error is highly recommended.

block.description:
Windows OS --- This data objects waits for CANCOM Manged Windows Cloud server deployment-system to complete. 
		
This can be used for dependency tracking purposes and returns in case of failure errors.  
The required `deployment_id` represents the id of `cancom_windows_os_deployment` resource.

Windows OS --- This data objects waits for CANCOM Manged Windows Cloud server deployment-system to complete. 
		
This can be used for dependency tracking purposes and returns in case of failure errors.  
The required `deployment_id` represents the id of `cancom_windows_os_deployment` resource.
